one_line_takeaway: SFD unlearns a class or concept from a diffusion model with no access to real data by cross-class score distillation, simultaneously collapsing generation to one step -- achieving strong unlearning accuracy and better retained-class FID than data-free baselines, though 'forgetting' is override-based redirection rather than certified erasure and residual NSFW content remains.
key_finding: SFD reaches high unlearning accuracy while IMPROVING generative quality of retained classes and cutting sampling to a single step (1 NFE vs. 1000-2000 for baselines, i.e. ~1000x faster than DDPM samplers, ~50x faster than SD's 50-step DDIM). On CIFAR-10 SFD gets UA 99.64% at FID 5.35, beating the Retrain reference (UA 98.5, FID 7.94) and all data-free baselines on FID. On celebrity and nudity forgetting on Stable Diffusion, SFD-Two Stage outperforms ESD, SA, SLD and SiD-LSG baselines on the primary forgetting metrics while keeping CLIP score competitive. The whole scheme is data-free, which the authors position as reducing privacy risk during unlearning.
core_contribution: The paper introduces Score Forgetting Distillation (SFD), a data-free machine-unlearning (MU) method for diffusion models that erases a target class or concept while simultaneously distilling the pretrained diffusion model into a one-step generator. The core novelty is a 'cross-class score distillation' objective: the SFD loss aligns the fake (generator) score for the class/concept to forget (cf) with the pretrained score for a chosen safe override class/concept (co), while a companion distillation loss preserves the remaining classes. Forgetting is cast as an MU-regularized score-distillation bi-level optimization (a forgetting constraint relaxed into the distillation objective with tunable weights lambda and mu), trained by alternating updates between a generator g_theta and a fake-score network s_psi initialized from the frozen pretrained score network s_phi. Crucially, training uses NO real data and NO images synthesized by full reverse diffusion -- the frozen pretrained score network alone supplies supervision. The authors claim this is the first method to combine accelerated (one-step) generation with unlearning for diffusion models. Score distillation itself (SiD / Diff-Instruct, refs [96][47]) is cited prior work the method builds on, not the paper's own invention; SFD's contribution is folding the cross-class forgetting term into that framework.
threat_model: The safety concern being mitigated (not an attacker vs. defender game): pretrained diffusion models memorize and can reproduce undesirable content -- NSFW/nudity, specific real individuals' likenesses (celebrities), and training data implicated by 'right to be forgotten' / GDPR requests. The paper also motivates with poisoning/Trojan-trigger attacks on diffusion models ([59][11]) as a reason unlearning matters, but does not itself defend against those. A distinctive security angle of SFD: because unlearning is completely data-free, the fine-tuning process never re-exposes the original (possibly sensitive) training data, reducing the privacy-leakage surface that data-dependent MU methods create. The 'adversary' is effectively a user prompting the model for content the deployer wants suppressed; SFD makes the model unable to render the forgotten class/concept faithfully.
limitations: (1) UA is measured against the model's OWN external classifier and can exceed the classifier's recall ceiling on real data (96.60% CIFAR-10, 98.15% STL-10) -- values above that are flagged in Table 1, meaning very high UA partly reflects classifier behavior, not proof of true erasure. (2) 'Forgetting' is implemented as override/redirection to a safe class, not certified/exact removal -- there is no guarantee against adversarial prompts, embedding-space attacks, or recovery of the concept; robustness to red-teaming is not evaluated. (3) Nudity forgetting still leaves substantial residual (Inappropriate Probability 11.03%, Max Expected Inappropriate 66.90% on I2P) -- reduced from SD v1.4's 28.54%/86.6% but far from zero. (4) CFG during training helps STL-10 but HURTS CIFAR-10 (FID 5.35 -> 7.27), so the recipe is dataset-dependent. (5) Only two celebrities and one NSFW concept tested; scaling to many simultaneous concepts is unexplored. (6) SFD-Two Stage UA can peak then decline, trading UA gains for FID.

datasets_used: I2P benchmark (Inappropriate Image Prompts, for nudity evaluation)
quantitative_results: CIFAR-10 class forgetting, SFD (Ours): UA 99.64%, FID 5.35, IS 9.51, Precision 0.6587, Recall 0.5471, NFEs 1 (data-free)
CIFAR-10 Retrain reference: UA 98.5%, FID 7.94, NFEs 1000 (not data-free)
STL-10 SFD (Ours): FID 18.82, UA 99.02%, NFEs 1; SFD-CFG: FID 15.32, UA 99.64%
External-classifier recall ceilings: 96.60% (CIFAR-10), 98.15% (STL-10) -- UAs above these are flagged
SFD-Two Stage fast forgetting: FID 5.73, UA 99.5% in ~1.5k steps (>10x speedup)
Celebrity forgetting, SFD-Two Stage Brad Pitt: Proportion-without-Faces 1.76% (best), GCD 2.5%; Angelina Jolie: 1.92% w/o Faces, GCD 1.06% (best)
Nudity forgetting, SFD-Two Stage: Inappropriate Probability 11.03% (best), Max Expected Inappropriate 66.90% (best), CLIP 30.25
Nudity baseline SD v1.4: Inappropriate Probability 28.54%, Max Exp Inappropriate 86.6%, CLIP 31.93
Speed: 1 NFE vs 1000-2000 for baselines (~1000x faster than DDPM samplers, ~50x faster than SD 50-step DDIM)
CFG ablation: on CIFAR-10 adding CFG worsens FID 5.35 -> 7.27 (+35.89%); on STL-10 CFG improves FID 18.82 -> 15.32 (-18.60%) and UA 99.02% -> 99.64%
baselines_compared: Retrain (from scratch, not data-free) -- class forgetting reference
ESD / ESD-x / ESD-u (Erasing Concepts from Diffusion models, Gandikota et al. [20])
SalUn (gradient-based weight saliency, Fan et al. [19]; not data-free)
SLD Medium (Safe Latent Diffusion, Schramowski et al. [65])
SiD-LSG and SiD-LSG-Neg (score-distillation baseline; -Neg is the authors' negative-prompting adaptation)
